data.description:
## Summary
A catastrophic intermittent failure of the enrich-prune concurrency test (`intended=250
persisted=68 lost=182`): an orphaned `.git/index.lock` (left by an abnormally-terminated git
under CI memory/FS pressure) wedges EVERY store write for 300s. `_reclaim_if_stale_index_lock`
(`src/rebar/_store/gitutil.py`) only reclaims a lock older than `_INDEX_LOCK_STALE_S=300`, but
`_with_index_lock_retry` waits only ~2s — so any orphan cascades ~5 minutes of `StoreError`
raises. Distinct from the Mode A object-DB single-loss (ticket 4f8d / change 747).

## Reproduction Steps
1. Plant a young (age-0) `.git/index.lock` in the tracker's worktree gitdir.
2. Call `stage_and_commit` (holds the write lock). Every write RAISES in ~2s with
   `fatal: Unable to create '…/index.lock': File exists`; the lock persists for 300s.
   (Confirmed locally; the real orphan source is a SIGKILL/OOM/FS-fault git termination on CI.)

**Expected:** a locked write self-heals (reclaims the orphan) — under the exclusive write lock
the lock is provably not a live peer's.
**Actual:** wedges every write for 300s → 73% write loss.

## Root Cause
The store's git write helpers run UNDER the exclusive `write_lock` (dual-window fcntl+mkdir),
which serializes ALL rebar git on a tracker across processes (verified: 0 natural index.lock
firings over ~2250 writes). So an `index.lock` present under the lock is provably an ORPHAN.
But `_reclaim_if_stale_index_lock` applies the conservative 300s age gate (correct only for
UNLOCKED callers, where a live peer might hold it), so a young orphan wedges everything.

## Fix
Add `force=True` to `_reclaim_if_stale_index_lock` (bypass the age gate, keep the device+inode
TOCTOU re-validation) threaded through `_with_index_lock_retry(force_reclaim=…)`; the four
locked git helpers (`_git_add`/`_git_commit`/`_git_rm`/`_git_commit_paths`) pass it. Under the
write lock a young orphan is reclaimed on the first retry → the write self-heals.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Under the write lock, a young orphan `index.lock` is reclaimed immediately (RED test:
  `test_orphan_index_lock_under_write_lock_self_heals`, mutation-checked).
- [ ] The UNLOCKED reclaim (`force=False`) still preserves a young lock (safety invariant kept).
- [ ] The concurrency test passes; make lint + typecheck pass; no existing test weakened.
data.title: Orphan .git/index.lock wedges all store writes for 300s under the write lock (enrich-prune Mode B cascade)
